mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in greater than fifty-five percent of months when PAYEMS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds five percent, restrictive monetary policy elevates real discount rates and corporate debt-service costs, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days as earnings expectations reset lower.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than fifty percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds five percent.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a federal funds rate above four and one-half percent alongside consumer price inflation accelerating more than one and one-half index points over three months sustains a restrictive real policy rate that compresses equity valuation multiples and depresses S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are non-negative in more than forty-five percent of joint-high observations.

mechanism_narrative: Deceleration in nonfarm payrolls during late-cycle economic expansions forecasts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because slowing employment growth signals impending demand contraction and corporate margin compression.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following payroll deceleration in late-cycle periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A three-month contraction in industrial production reflects weakening goods-sector output and inventory drawdowns that precede downward earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than fifty percent of INDPRO contraction episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below one thousand four hundred fifty thousand units signals deteriorating residential investment and construction employment, transmitting into lower aggregate demand and negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in fewer than fifty-five percent of triggered instances when HOUST is below 1450.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated unemployment above four percent and consumer price inflation accelerating more than one index point over three months reduce real household disposable income and raise equity risk premiums through stagflationary fears, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are non-negative in more than fifty percent of joint-triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer spending that supports corporate top-line revenue expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following RSAFS growth months are not positive in greater than fifty-five percent of observations.
